event.summary: Claimed one ready iteration unit without expanding repository or path scope.

acceptance.proof: Both named directions terminate under explicit process, discovery, connection, and sample time bounds and publish one typed sanitized outcome each.

Pinned official package/runtime/binary and independent driver/raw evidence hashes are present while raw packets, XML, endpoints, diagnostics, binaries, environments, caches, and private paths remain absent.
No production source, protocol correction, other format, multicast/non-loopback, device behavior, unsafe/FFI, or Manifold authority is added.

document.commit_policy: Commit only sanitized derived observation evidence, provenance, validators, and routing; keep raw packets, XML, endpoints, diagnostics, binaries, environments, caches, and drivers private; keep lifecycle and push commits intentional.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the exact two-direction observation and strict nonclaims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run focused, provenance, privacy, and owner gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Report only sanitized interoperability outcomes without broad compatibility claims.
Separate each official/Rust direction and observed stage from implementation claims.
Bind official and private evidence hashes without raw publication.
Route finite process/discovery/connection/sample bounds and boundary scans.
Review clean-room public/private and direct-network routing; no shared mutation expected.
Review data-plane observation versus authority ownership; no shared mutation expected.

non_scope: Official implementation source, copied or translated source, production Rust changes, protocol correction, discovery responder ownership, multicast/interface selection, non-loopback reachability, authentication, performance, currentness, or ecosystem-wide compatibility.
Additional channel counts, formats, chunks, clock correction, queues, recovery, FFI, devices, or adapters.

Raw packets, XML, endpoint values, diagnostics, binaries, environments, caches, local paths, device identities, or Manifold admission, registry, session, route, topology, lease, authorization, replay, or audit authority.
document.objective: Observe the accepted Rust timestamped single-channel Float32 runtime against the pinned official protocol-110 endpoint in two separately bounded IPv4-loopback casesâ€”Rust outlet to official inlet and official outlet to Rust inletâ€”and publish only sanitized typed success or failure outcomes with exact provenance.
outputs: Two finite IPv4-loopback-only black-box cases using the accepted Rust runtime and pinned official public APIs without official implementation-source inspection.
Sanitized typed outcome per direction retaining exact version/binary/driver/raw-evidence hashes but no raw bytes, XML, endpoint values, diagnostics, environment, cache, or local path.
A bounded conclusion that either records exact sample/timestamp compatibility or identifies the smallest observed failure stage for a later correction unit.
